Trust model:   - Only addresses with ISSUER_ROLE may issue or revoke credentials.   - Issuers must be approved by governance (GovernanceModule).   - Schemas must be registered and active before credentials can reference them.   - A revocation accumulator enables efficient batch revocation checks.","errors":{"AccessControlBadConfirmation()":[{"details":"The caller of a function is not the expected one. NOTE: Don't confuse with {AccessControlUnauthorizedAccount}."}],"AccessControlUnauthorizedAccount(address,bytes32)":[{"details":"The `account` is missing a role."}],"EnforcedPause()":[{"details":"The operation failed because the contract is paused."}],"ExpectedPause()":[{"details":"The operation failed because the contract is not paused."}],"ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall()":[{"details":"Unauthorized reentrant call."}]},"events":{"Paused(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the pause is triggered by `account`."},"RoleAdminChanged(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32)":{"details":"Emitted when `newAdminRole` is set as ``role``'s admin role, replacing `previousAdminRole` `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` is the starting admin for all roles, despite {RoleAdminChanged} not being emitted to signal this."},"RoleGranted(bytes32,address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when `account` is granted `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call. This account bears the admin role (for the granted role). Expected in cases where the role was granted using the internal {AccessControl-_grantRole}."},"RoleRevoked(bytes32,address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when `account` is revoked `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call:   - if using `revokeRole`, it is the admin role bearer   - if using `renounceRole`, it is the role bearer (i.e. `account`)"},"Unpaused(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the pause is lifted by `account`."}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"approveSchema(bytes32)":{"params":{"schemaHash":"The schema hash to approve"}},"batchCheckValidity(bytes32[])":{"params":{"credentialHashes":"Array of credential hashes to check"},"returns":{"results":"Array of validity booleans"}},"batchIssueCredentials(bytes32[],bytes32[],bytes32[],uint64[],bytes32[])":{"params":{"credentialHashes":"Array of credential hashes","expiresAts":"Array of expiry timestamps","merkleRoots":"Array of Merkle roots for credential data","schemaHashes":"Array of schema hashes","subjectDids":"Array of subject DID hashes"}},"constructor":{"params":{"_identityRegistry":"Address of the ZeroID identity registry","admin":"Initial admin address"}},"getRoleAdmin(bytes32)":{"details":"Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}."},"grantRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event."},"hasRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Returns `true` if `account` has been granted `role`."},"isRevokedInBitmap(bytes32)":{"params":{"credentialHash":"The credential to check"},"returns":{"_0":"True if the credential is revoked in the bitmap"}},"paused()":{"details":"Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise."},"renounceRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"revokeRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"supportsInterface(bytes4)":{"details":"Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas."}},"stateVariables":{"_approvedSchemas":{"details":"Approved schemas: schemaHash => active flag"},"_credentials":{"details":"credentialHash => Credential"},"_issuerCredentials":{"details":"Issuer DID => list of credential hashes they have issued"},"_subjectCredentials":{"details":"Subject DID => list of credential hashes held"},"currentRevocationEpoch":{"details":"Revocation accumulator: epoch-based bitmap for efficient revocation checksepoch => slot => bitmap (each bit = one credential in the set)"}},"title":"CredentialRegistry","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"advanceRevocationEpoch()":{"notice":"Advance the revocation epoch (governance only)"},"approveSchema(bytes32)":{"notice":"Approve a credential schema (called by governance)"},"batchCheckValidity(bytes32[])":{"notice":"Batch-check validity of multiple credentials"},"batchIssueCredentials(bytes32[],bytes32[],bytes32[],uint64[],bytes32[])":{"notice":"Batch-issue multiple credentials in a single transaction"},"getIssuerCredentials(bytes32)":{"notice":"Get all credential hashes issued by a specific issuer"},"getSubjectCredentials(bytes32)":{"notice":"Get all credential hashes held by a subject"},"identityRegistry()":{"notice":"ZeroID identity registry for DID validation"},"isRevokedInBitmap(bytes32)":{"notice":"Check if a credential is revoked using the bitmap"},"isSchemaApproved(bytes32)":{"notice":"Check if a schema is approved"},"revokeSchema(bytes32)":{"notice":"Revoke a credential schema"},"totalCredentialsIssued()":{"notice":"Total credentials issued"},"totalRevoked()":{"notice":"Total credentials currently revoked"}},"notice":"Manages the full credential lifecycle \u2014 issuance, suspension,         reinstatement, revocation, and expiry \u2014 for the ZeroID protocol.         Credentials are stored as Merkle-root commitments; actual claim         data never touches the params.credentialHashes: Array of credential hashes to check

params.merkleRoots: Array of Merkle roots for credential data

params._identityRegistry: Address of the ZeroID identity registry

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

returns._0: True if the credential is revoked in the bitmap

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

approveSchema(bytes32).notice: Approve a credential schema (called by governance)

batchIssueCredentials(bytes32[],bytes32[.notice: Batch-issue multiple credentials in a single transaction

getIssuerCredentials(bytes32).notice: Get all credential hashes issued by a specific issuer

getSubjectCredentials(bytes32).notice: Get all credential hashes held by a subject

identityRegistry().notice: ZeroID identity registry for DID validation

isRevokedInBitmap(bytes32).notice: Check if a credential is revoked using the bitmap

isSchemaApproved(bytes32).notice: Check if a schema is approved
